title: Holly Kallmeyer
content:
People.ContactEmail hkallmeyer@mccarthy.ca
- University of Toronto
Holly is an associate in our Litigation Group in Toronto. She maintains a general litigation practice with a focus on complex commercial disputes, class actions, and public law. Holly has argued before the Ontario Superior Court of Justice and the Court of Appeal for Ontario, and has appeared before the Supreme Court of Canada.
Some of Holly’s representative engagements include:
- Second chair on a three-week civil jury trial, which resulted in a successful verdict for a physician accused of negligence
- Counsel for a major mining company in an arbitration regarding claims for fair value and oppression arising from the purchase of a publicly traded mining company
- Counsel for a provincial Attorney General in a class action concerning allegations of institutional abuse
- Counsel for a provincial Attorney General in a Charter application concerning wage restraint legislation
- Counsel for a cannabis company in an arbitration arising from the sale of retail cannabis stores
- Counsel for a financial institution in a class action regarding consumer loans
- Counsel for a biotechnology company in a class action regarding premature commercialization of a genetically modified seed
- Counsel for a major telecommunications company in a variety of matters, including appeals to the Federal Court of Appeal of decisions of the Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission
- Counsel for financial institutions in criminal matters, including production orders
- Counsel for multiple intervenors before the Supreme Court of Canada
Holly graduated from the University of Toronto Faculty of Law with Honours and received the Michael J. Moldaver Prize for highest standing in third year. Holly was a senior editor of the Faculty’s Law Review, a member of the Moot Court Committee, and a student coordinator at Pro Bono Ontario. She mooted in the Gale Cup and the Faculty’s Grand Moot. After articling with McCarthy Tétrault, Holly clerked for Associate Chief Justice Fairburn and Justices Gillese, Hoy, and Huscroft of the Court of Appeal for Ontario. Holly also holds a MPhil with Distinction in International Relations and Politics from the University of Cambridge and a BAH with Distinction in Political Studies from Queen’s University.
Holly was called to the bar in 2020. She is a member of the Law Society of Ontario and The Advocates’ Society.
- The Supreme Court reaffirms the provinces' power to regulate cannabis April 14, 2023
- Publication Defending global claims at home: rebalancing transnational mining litigation in Ontario May 19, 2026
- Blog Post Ontario’s Top Court Confirms that Section 11(c) of the Charter Applies to Civil Contempt Proceedings April 4, 2025
- Blog Post The Court of Appeal Decides that One Bad Apple Spoils the Bunch: A reasonable apprehension of bias towards one arbitrator renders the tribunal’s decision void February 10, 2025
- Blog Post The Supreme Court Rules on the Provinces' Power to Regulate Cannabis May 1, 2023
- Publication Virtual Advocacy: Litigating from a Distance February 28, 2023
